subject: Ecology
title: Stormwater Runoff
description: A fast breakdown of Stormwater Runoff for builders.

hook.text: When rain hits a forest, the soil absorbs it. When rain hits a city, it runs off.

buildup.text: Impervious surfaces—roads, rooftops, parking lots—prevent water from soaking into the ground.

discovery.text: Runoff picks up oil, metals, and fertilizers, carrying them straight into rivers and lakes.

twist.text: A 1-inch rainstorm in a city generates 16 times more runoff than the same storm in a forest.

climax.text: Green infrastructure—rain gardens, permeable pavement—mimics natural absorption.

punchline.text: Pavement is the problem. Soil is the solution.

quiz.question: Why do cities have more stormwater runoff?
options: Urban soil absorbs too much water
